format_discipline.notes: No frontmatter. Opens with # heading. Sidecar reading.meta.json present. Six required sections present in correct order: Overview, Key Concepts, Worked Example, In Practice, Key Takeaways, References.

length.notes: 1599 words — 1 word under the 1600 ceiling per meta; within tolerance. Estimated 6.95 min at 230 wpm, within 6–8 min target.

citations.notes: [1] Marda & Narayan arXiv:2205.05661 cited 5 times. [2] HES Fintech / ECOA cited 3 times. [3] Grant Thornton 2026 AI Impact Survey cited 2 times. All inline markers resolve to bibliography entries; no orphan markers or missing references.

forward_references.notes: automation bias (9.5) used without re-definition. Judgment Framework Q3 attributed to topic 9.9 explicitly. pipeline, training data (10.1) and failure mode (10.2) reused without re-definition. New terms — diffuse accountability, accountability gap, model documentation, adverse action notice — all defined on first use. No forward references to topics beyond 10.3.

faithfulness.notes: All 10 sampled claims trace directly to corpus §5.1–§6. Pipeline four-stage description, accountability three-component table, diffuse accountability and accountability gap definitions, Q3 'nobody knows' framing, ECOA specificity requirement, model documentation and adverse action notice descriptions, 78% executive statistic, and six-step audit all match corpus exactly or with appropriate condensation. No hallucinations detected. Minor omission: corpus remediation definition includes 'compensating the person harmed'; reading omits this clause — condensation within acceptable range, not a faithfulness failure.

beginner_readability.notes: ECOA spelled out in full on first use. All new terms bolded and defined inline. Concrete scenario in Overview anchors the abstract framework. No jargon without explanation.

formatting.notes: Accountability table in Key Concepts, numbered lists for pipeline and six-step audit, bullets in Key Takeaways. No unbroken walls of text. Diagram embedded with alt text and figure caption.

scope.notes: Stays on accountability in AI lending throughout. No credit scoring mathematics or model mechanics taught. Assessment-prep language from corpus §4 correctly excluded from reading artifact.

observations: Word count at upper boundary (1599/1600) — within tolerance, no action required.

Corpus Remediation component includes 'and for compensating the person harmed'; reading condenses to 'A mechanism for correcting the decision if it was wrong.' Acceptable abbreviation for reading length target.

Diffuse accountability in Key Concepts lists three pipeline actors (bank, vendor, data provider); corpus §5.3 lists five (adds cloud infrastructure provider and regulator). Omission is appropriate for standard profile — the three named actors are the analytically salient ones and the six-step audit covers the broader enumeration.
